content:
finding: `<` boundary at `expMs == nowMs` rejects, causes 401 loop when skew budget not applied uniformly
exploit: token at exact expiry rejected; refresh issues new token; next compare again hits boundary; loop
invariant: expiry check uses `expMs + skewMs <= nowMs` => `401`
invariant: skew budget = 30s, single constant, single site
invariant: ms normalization before compare
mitigation: replace `expMs < nowMs` with `(expMs + SKEW_MS) <= nowMs`
mitigation: define `SKEW_MS = 30_000` in `auth/` once
mitigation: keep `next(err)` as single error path
mitigation: no change to refresh logic
verification: unit `expMs == nowMs` => pass (within skew)
verification: unit `expMs + 30_000 == nowMs` => pass (exact boundary)
verification: unit `expMs + 30_001 <= nowMs` => `401`
verification: unit missing token => `401`
verification: unit malformed payload => `401`
verification: unit seconds-vs-ms mismatch => `401`, no silent accept
verification: integration no refresh loop at exact expiry
risk: widening skew beyond 30s if constant reused loosely
risk: other modules still using `<` inconsistently
risk: clock source drift between gateway and service
answer: patch single compare site in `auth/` expiry middleware; add `SKEW_MS`; add 6 boundary tests

A: modular_monolith
why: team(6) ∧ ddl(4 months) ∧ ops(pt_devops) ∧ traffic(modest) ⇒ low_ops_cost
store: PostgreSQL
seams: billing/ | core_app/ | auth/ | gateway/
split: defer → post_release
R: ! premature_distribution

Sign up for free to join this conversation on GitHub. Already have an account? Sign in to comment

finding: public boundary trusts `x-user-id`, overrides `session.userId`
severity: high
class: session confusion / horizontal privesc

exploit:
attacker sends `x-user-id: <victim>` header
middleware picks header over session
downstream scopes to victim id
reads/writes victim data
`401` only fires when both absent, not when spoofed

invariant broken:
`session.userId` authoritative at public edge
`x-user-id` not authoritative at public edge

mitigation:
revert to `const userId = session.userId;`
drop `x-user-id` at `gateway/` for public traffic
if internal proxy needs it, verify signed gateway assertion in `auth/`, never OR-fallback to session
keep `401` on missing session

verify:
test: spoofed `x-user-id` + valid session of user A → scope = A, header ignored
test: spoofed `x-user-id`, no session → `401`
test: no session, no header → `401`
test: cross-user denial — user A session cannot read user B record via header
test: gateway strips client-supplied `x-user-id` on public route

risks:
! silent privesc in production until patched
! audit logs attribute actions to wrong user
? internal callers relying on header — confirm via `gateway/` contract before removal
? mobile/analytics clients sending header — check telemetry

@flint v0 hybrid
@cb[
 μ1=loadUser;
 α1=async/await;
 ε1=next(err);
 σ1=audit.log;
 σ2=cache.set;
 ι=missing_id
]

G: refactor(μ1, α1)
C: preserve(order) ∧ preserve(ε1) ∧ minimal_diff ∧ no_sig_change
P:
 1. validate(ι) ⇒ ε1(400)
 2. try: user=await db.findUser(id)
 3. catch: ε1(db_err)
 4. ¬user ⇒ ε1(404)
 5. await σ1(user)
 6. await σ2(user)
 7. req.user=user → next()
R:
 ! this_binding(db.findUser) → bind or promisify
 ! double_next if ε1 + next() both fire
 ! σ1/σ2 non-Promise → wrap
 ! order_drift on refactor
V:
 unit: ok_path ∧ ι_missing ∧ db_reject ∧ not_found ∧ σ1_reject ∧ σ2_reject
 assert: next_called_once ∧ ε1_single_site
Q: db.findUser Promise-native ?

---

Audit:
- Wrap μ1 as async; keep ε1 as single error sink.
- Order: ι check → db.findUser → not-found → σ1 → σ2 → next().
- Promisify db.findUser only if callback-only; watch this-binding.
- Route all failures via ε1; never
